case.title: SEACAT MARINE DRILLING CO. v. BABINEAUX

opinion.h: SEACAT MARINE DRILLING CO. v. BABINEAUX, 382 U.S. 16 (1965)
SEACAT MARINE DRILLING CO. ET AL. v. BABINEAUX. APPEAL FROM THE COURT OF APPEAL OF LOUISIANA, THIRD CIRCUIT. No. 283. Decided October 11, 1965.
opinion.p: The appeal is dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
MR. JUSTICE HARLAN is of the opinion that the appeal should be dismissed for want of jurisdiction.
opinion.h: BERRY v. STATE TAX COMMISSION, 382 U.S. 16 (1965)
opinion.p: 382 U.S. 16 BERRY v. STATE TAX COMMISSION. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF OREGON. No. 229. Decided October 11, 1965.
Robert Y. Thornton, Attorney General of Oregon, and John C. Mull and Carlisle B. Roberts, Assistant Attorneys General, for appellee.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
MR. JUSTICE HARLAN is of the opinion that probable jurisdiction should be noted.
